constraints: PyPI rate limits, project availability, and service policies still apply
Distribution downloads and core metadata are bounded by the selected byte limits
Full-text web search is served only through a browser challenge and is intentionally not mapped
human_boundaries: Account creation, login, passkeys, and two-factor challenges require the user's credentials and human authorization.
Adding or removing project owners and maintainers grants publication authority and requires privileged account access.
Changing account email, profile, or password requires the authenticated account holder.
Configuring TOTP or WebAuthn two-factor devices requires the account holder and cannot be automated.
Creating or revoking API tokens exposes publishing credentials and requires authenticated human authorization.
Deleting a project is destructive and requires project ownership plus explicit human confirmation.
Organization members, teams, roles, and billing require organization-owner access unavailable to the public matrix.
PyPI's full-text web search is protected by a browser challenge; programmatic discovery uses list_all_projects and the JSON metadata API.
Uploading a distribution changes the public Python supply chain and requires an authenticated API token plus narrowly scoped human confirmation.
Yanking or deleting a release changes what users install and requires project ownership plus explicit confirmation.
coverage.website_parity: All anonymous public PyPI JSON, simple-index, integrity, RSS, statistics, and informational surfaces in the declared en-US matrix have reviewed browserless operations. Publishing, project and account management, and web full-text search remain declared human or authenticated boundaries outside that matrix.

description: Complete browserless interface for PyPI's anonymous public API surface: project and release metadata, versions, dependencies, distribution files and downloads, PEP 658 core metadata, PEP 740 provenance, vulnerabilities, ownership, discovery, RSS feeds, storage statistics, and informational pages.

get_project.summary: Fetch a public PyPI project's normalized metadata: latest version, summary, license, classifiers, project URLs, owners, and release count.
get_project.when_not_to_use: Do not use this to enumerate every historical version; list_releases and list_versions are bounded and paginated.
get_project.when_to_use: Use this first when the project name is known to read its current metadata and latest distribution files.

read_site_resource.summary: Read a bounded public pypi.org informational page (help, policy, or project page) as visible text and links.

assertions.name: site resource read returns bounded visible text

read_site_resource.when_not_to_use: Do not use it for full-text web search, which is protected by a browser challenge; use list_all_projects instead.
read_site_resource.when_to_use: Use this for PyPI help, policy, and project pages that are not covered by the JSON API.
